items.body: The John Deere Classic is back in the Quad Cities — that easygoing July stop at TPC Deere Run where Midwest summers and loud, friendly galleries have been a midsummer tradition for more than fifty years. Jordan Spieth won the first of his PGA Tour titles here in 2013 and returns as a two-time champion; defending champ Brian Campbell and Rickie Fowler headline another starry field before everyone turns toward The Open. Expect birdies, big roars, and the kind of small-town warmth you don't get every week.

items.label: Ben Griffin (+1300 to +1400):
items.body: One of the hottest players on Tour after three wins in 2025, and he already has a top-five finish here.

items.label: Chris Gotterup (+1375 to +1400):
items.body: A fast-rising name near the top of the board who can go low when the putter gets rolling.

items.label: Keith Mitchell (+1750 to +2200):
items.body: A proven winner whose steady game fits a tree-lined Midwest track.

items.label: Keegan Bradley (+2500 to +2600):
items.body: A major champion and Ryder Cup regular who can make a run when the scores go low.

items.label: J.T. Poston (+2700 to +2800):
items.body: The 2022 winner here who knows every corner of TPC Deere Run.

items.label: Jordan Spieth (+2800 to +3300):
items.body: Two-time champ in Silvis with deep Quad Cities history — this is where his PGA Tour story began.

items.label: Jackson Koivun (+2000 to +2800):
items.body: The standout amateur from last year turns pro with plenty of local buzz.

items.label: Michael Thorbjornsen (+3200):
items.body: Nearly won here in 2024 and has the length to take advantage of a gettable setup.

items.label: Rickie Fowler (+3600):
items.body: Always a fan favorite and a threat when the short game is sharp.

items.label: Davis Thompson (+4500 to +5100):
items.body: The 2024 champ who went an absurd 28-under — still very much in the conversation on familiar turf.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois, in the Quad Cities region along the Illinois-Iowa border.

items.label: Established:
items.body: 1971; the 2026 edition is the 55th John Deere Classic on the PGA Tour.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Brian Campbell won in 2025 at 18-under, one year after Davis Thompson's record-setting 28-under victory.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: A beloved Midwest summer stop tied to the John Deere brand, with strong local support, charity impact, and a reputation as one of the Tour's most fan-friendly events.

items.label: Course:
items.body: TPC Deere Run, Silvis, Illinois.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 2 through July 5, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $8.8 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 144-player field, 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A 7,327-yard par-71 D.A. Weibring and Chris Gray design that plays soft and scorable in summer, rewarding precise wedge play and hot putters, with tree-lined fairways and a redesigned fourth hole that adds bunkering and length after the removal of the Hewitt Tree.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday on Golf Channel (4-7 p.m. ET); weekend rounds on Golf Channel (1-3 p.m. ET) and CBS (3-6 p.m. ET Saturday and Sunday).

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 2 through July 5, 2026.
